editorialNotes: v26.5.911-dev ships the Settings inner-list filtering optimization from PR #562 on top of the v26.5.910-dev installed soak baseline.

release.deck: Faster Settings feed filtering, relaxed hidden renderer watchdog, and same-day desktop fixes
fixes: Settings feed management now precomputes normalized list search text and memoizes feed extraction, keeping 1,600-feed inner filters in single-digit milliseconds.
Settings drops shell backdrop filtering only while the dialog is moving, reducing interaction paint work while restoring the blurred background at rest.
Settings scroll now uses scrollend for scrollspy updates and simplifies moving-state shadows, reducing paint work while preserving the blurred backdrop.
Large feed browsing now filters active scope and verifies priority order in one pass, reducing startup work for 5,000-item libraries while preserving feed URL source views.
Feed and Map location scans now prefilter text before running location regexes, avoiding needless regex work on ordinary article text.

Settings now avoids forcing its scroll container into a promoted compositor layer and keeps the dialog shell blur stable during movement, reducing compositor churn while preserving the blurred backdrop.
Settings avoids repeated scroll timer churn and uses visibility observation for update auto-checks, reducing interaction work while the dialog is moving.
Expected hidden-window WebKit timer throttling now records renderer_heartbeat_throttled instead of renderer_heartbeat_stale, so installed-build soaks stop reporting fake stale cycles.
Renderer heartbeats now include active surface plus Friends graph and Map counts, making installed build soaks attribute memory and frame issues to the visible product area.
Friends Fit all now keeps sparse graph nodes visible instead of clamping zoom too high for far-apart people.
Dense Map views now mount 160 markers instead of 240 and only paint 80 while moving, giving the 1,600-author Map guard more frame headroom.
Feed cards now shed inline media when aggregate WebKit RSS reaches 1.5 GB.
Shows the fatal recovery screen instead of a blank shell when a React render error is caught.
Restores WebKit WebContent memory attribution for current launch renderer processes.
Relaxes hidden renderer watchdog recovery so hidden or backgrounded windows are not recycled too aggressively.
followUps: Continue the installed v26.5.911-dev soak and compare WebKit RSS against the v26.5.910 baseline.
Continue Map, Friends, and Settings interaction monitoring under real desktop load.
Investigate the remaining Settings outer-scroll p95 noise, which still appears without JavaScript long tasks.
Keeps hidden sessions quieter and restores WebKit memory attribution.
Improves dense Friends graph recovery, Map interaction smoothness, desktop crash recovery, and WebKit memory attribution for today's dev builds.
Reduces dense Map marker work, speeds Settings, and sheds feed media earlier under WebKit pressure.
Investigate the remaining Settings scroll p95 noise, which still appears without JavaScript long tasks.

releaseBody:
(AI Generated).

## Freed v26.5.911-dev

Faster Settings feed filtering, relaxed hidden renderer watchdog, and same-day desktop fixes

### Fixes

- Reader, feed, and header polish
- Settings avoids repeated scroll timer churn and uses visibility observation for update auto-checks, reducing interaction work while the dialog is moving
- Release build and type-safety cleanup

### Follow-ups

- Continue the installed v26.5.911-dev soak and compare WebKit RSS against the v26.5.910 baseline
- Continue Map, Friends, and Settings interaction monitoring under real desktop load
- Investigate the remaining Settings outer-scroll p95 noise, which still appears without JavaScript long tasks
- Stabilize map markers across refreshes
- Keeps hidden sessions quieter and restores WebKit memory attribution
- Improves dense Friends graph recovery, Map interaction smoothness, desktop crash recovery, and WebKit memory attribution for today's dev builds
- Reduces dense Map marker work, speeds Settings, and sheds feed media earlier under WebKit pressure

### Downloads

**macOS:** `.dmg` (Apple Silicon or Intel, signed + notarized)  
**Windows:** `.exe` (NSIS installer)  
**Linux:** `.AppImage`  

> macOS downloads are signed and notarized for normal Gatekeeper installation.
